evals.name: scan-project-dependencies
evals.description: User wants to scan a TypeScript/Node.js project for dependency vulnerabilities
evals.prompt: Hey, can you run osv-scanner on this project? We've got a Node.js project with package-lock.json in the root. I want to find any known CVEs in our npm dependencies. Give me a JSON report.
evals.expected_output: JSON vulnerability report showing any known CVEs in the node dependencies
expectations: The skill uses the Podman container (ghcr.io/google/osv-scanner:latest)
The skill mounts the workspace to /src with :Z SELinux flag
The skill runs scan source -r --format json
The skill outputs the JSON results to stdout or a file
The skill explains exit codes (0 = pass, 1 = vulns found)

evals.name: scan-container-image
evals.description: User wants to scan a container image for vulnerabilities using exported archive
evals.prompt: We need to scan our production container image 'myapp:latest' for vulnerabilities. We're using Podman and don't want to mount the Docker socket. What's the best way to scan it with osv-scanner?
evals.expected_output: Instructions for exporting the image with podman save and scanning the tar archive
expectations: The skill recommends using the archive method (podman save --format=docker-archive + --archive flag)
The skill explains why archive is preferred over socket mount
The skill provides the correct podman save command
The skill provides the correct podman run command for scanning the archive

evals.name: license-compliance-check
evals.description: User wants to check license compliance in their project
evals.prompt: I need to check if all our dependencies have approved licenses. We only allow MIT, Apache-2.0, and BSD-3-Clause licenses. Can you use osv-scanner to check our project at /home/user/project?
evals.expected_output: License compliance check command with the specified SPDX allowlist
expectations: The skill uses the --licenses flag with the allowlist
The skill correctly specifies the SPDX identifiers
The skill runs via the Podman container
The skill explains how to interpret the license summary output

evals.name: offline-scanning-setup
evals.description: User wants to set up offline vulnerability scanning for air-gapped environments
evals.prompt: Our CI runners don't have internet access. How can I set up osv-scanner to scan dependencies offline? I need steps for downloading the database once and then scanning without network.
evals.expected_output: Step-by-step offline scanning setup with database download and offline scan commands
expectations: The skill explains the --offline-vulnerabilities flag
The skill explains the --download-offline-databases flag
The skill explains the OSV_SCANNER_LOCAL_DB_CACHE_DIRECTORY env var
The skill provides commands for both downloading and scanning offline
The skill mentions mounting a persistent cache volume for the database

evals.name: sbom-generation
evals.description: User wants to generate an SBOM for compliance
evals.prompt: We need to generate an SPDX SBOM of all our project dependencies for compliance reporting. Can osv-scanner do that? Our project has multiple lockfiles (package-lock.json, Cargo.lock, go.mod).
evals.expected_output: SBOM generation command using osv-scanner with SPDX or CycloneDX format
expectations: The skill uses --all-packages and --format spdx-2-3 or cyclonedx-1-5
The skill explains where to save the SBOM output
The skill notes that SPDX output does not include vulnerability data
The skill mentions CycloneDX as an alternative with vulnerability support
